body.title: Granny: enforce cc_max_concurrent=1 before dispatch
body.result: GRANNY_THROTTLED channel post added to run_once(); gate was already in code but fired silently
body.description:
Granny dispatched 6 concurrent CC sessions on 2026-06-01, consuming 96% context and losing ~4h of work. Before calling _launch_cc_instance(), check tmux list-sessions for sessions matching 'cc-T-*'. If count >= cc_max_concurrent (config, default 1), post GRANNY_THROTTLED to channel and return False instead of dispatching. Also: after killing a CC session (orphan reset), verify it's actually dead before marking ticket back to sprint.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/dispatch.py, devices/granny/daemon.py (config), devices/granny/config.py (or daemon config dict)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; log every state change; structural rules — OOP-first, device.py is design center
**Scope boundary:** IN: check-before-dispatch guard in cc_dispatch_fn; cc_max_concurrent config key; GRANNY_THROTTLED channel event. OUT: web circuit breakers (separate ticket), orphan watchdog (T-granny-orphan-watchdog).
**Completion criteria:** `tmux list-sessions | grep cc-T-` returning 1 existing session → second dispatch attempt posts GRANNY_THROTTLED to channel and returns False. Test in tests/granny/ verifying the guard. No new tmux session spawned when limit reached.
